title: Regional and social differences concerning overweight, participation in health check-ups and vaccination. Analysis of data from a whole birth cohort of 6-year old children in a prosperous German city.
ehr_evidence: data of the medical check-up prior to school enrolment
summary: Analysis of regional and social differences concerning overweight, participation in health check-ups, and vaccination among 6-year-old children in Munich, Germany.
key_findings: Large variation between school districts concerning overweight, health check-ups, and vaccinations
Mother tongue of parents is the most pronounced association with overweight and low check-up rates
Regional level influences are small when individual level social variables are controlled for
captured_features: percentage of households with low educational level
study_design: multilevel analysis of a birth cohort
limitations: limited number and scope of variables
lack of information on community and health care services
lack of information on social networks
lack of information on traffic noise and air pollution
